frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
generated_comment: Yaar this is exactly the problem, every few weeks some woman-related issue comes up and client work suffers. I get that they have genuine problems, but in this kind of role we need people who can be consistently available na.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it generalizes female colleagues as less reliable due to menstruation or similar gendered health issues. It encodes workplace bias by framing women as inherently unsuitable for demanding client-facing roles because of their bodies.
persona_text: You are Ankit. Age 29. A software developer in Pune. You are in a one on one WhatsApp chat with your closest male colleague Saurabh. Your female team member Riya has an important client call today but your manager just told you to reassign it because Riya called in sick again due to Ôwoman-relatedÕ issue. You are telling Saurabh why you think this keeps happening with female colleagues.
